title: Still the Noise (Chitta Vritti Nirodhah)
core_intention: Practice settling mental agitation and returning to inner quiet.
closing_prompt: Notice what changes when you stop chasing every thought.

title: Steady Effort Over Time (Abhyasa)
core_intention: Build trust in consistent practice, not perfection.
closing_prompt: Progress happens when you show up again and again.

title: Letting Go of Grasping (Vairagya)
core_intention: Practice non-attachment to outcomes, sensations, and performance.
closing_prompt: You don’t have to hold tightly to feel safe.

title: Reality vs. Story (Pramana + Viparyaya + Vikalpa)
core_intention: See clearly what is true, what is distorted, and what is imagined.
closing_prompt: Can you feel the difference between facts and fear?

title: Skillful Rest (Yoga Nidra + Conscious Recovery)
core_intention: Honor rest as a practice that supports clarity and healing.
closing_prompt: Rest is not laziness. It’s wisdom.

title: The Observer Within (Drashtu / Witness Consciousness)
core_intention: Strengthen the ability to witness experiences without becoming them.
closing_prompt: You are not the storm—you are the sky noticing it.

title: Purifying the System (Kriya Yoga)
core_intention: Use heat, study, and surrender to clean out what blocks growth.
closing_prompt: What if your effort is cleansing, not punishing?

title: Working With the Kleshas (The Roots of Suffering)
core_intention: Recognize the inner patterns that generate stress and reactivity.
closing_prompt: We can’t heal what we refuse to notice.

title: The Ethics of Freedom (Yamas)
core_intention: Build inner peace through how you treat others and yourself.
closing_prompt: Peace isn’t just what you feel—it’s what you practice.

title: Inner Habits That Heal (Niyamas)
core_intention: Strengthen inner steadiness through daily self-care + devotion.
closing_prompt: Your habits shape your nervous system—and your destiny.

title: Energy Mastery (Pranayama)
core_intention: Practice regulating life force through breath rhythm and control.
closing_prompt: Your breath is the remote control for your nervous system.

title: Turning Inward (Pratyahara)
core_intention: Withdraw attention from external noise and reclaim inner space.
closing_prompt: Your inner world is a sanctuary you can return to anytime.

title: One-Pointed Mind (Dharana)
core_intention: Train the attention to hold steady on one object, anchor, or feeling.
closing_prompt: The mind becomes powerful when it stops scattering.

title: Meditation as a Skill (Dhyana)
core_intention: Move from doing yoga to becoming absorbed in awareness itself.
closing_prompt: Meditation isn’t emptying the mind—it’s meeting it with steadiness.

title: Liberation Through Non-Identification (Kaivalya)
core_intention: Practice freeing the self from being controlled by mental/emotional forces.
closing_prompt: What remains when you stop clinging to who you think you are?
